last_message: 8 VCs found (live search or model knowledge).

pins.name: FirstMark Capital
pins.sector_focus: Consumer Tech, Digital Health, Behavioral Health
pins.compatibility_summary: FirstMark Capital is an excellent fit because they invest in consumer tech and digital health startups, including behavioral health solutions like Ro. Their focus on early-stage companies aligns with seed-stage funding needs, and their New York base accommodates US geography.

pins.name: SV Health Investors
pins.compatibility_summary: SV Health Investors specializes in healthcare with a strong focus on digital health and behavioral health, making them ideal for an accountability tech solution targeting resolution failures. Their stage focus and check size match well with early-stage startups needing $1-3M seed funding.

pins.name: OMERS Ventures
pins.stage_focus: Series A, Series B, Series C
pins.compatibility_summary: OMERS Ventures has invested in behavioral health providers like Caraway and HelloSelf, showing clear interest in solutions that improve adherence and accountability. While they typically invest at Series A and beyond, their focus on behavioral health technology makes them a strong candidate for later-stage follow-on funding.

pins.name: Pear VC
pins.compatibility_summary: Pear VC has a proven track record in healthcare investments including Guardant Health and has shown interest in consumer-facing health solutions. Their investment thesis aligns well with accountability tech that improves user outcomes through behavioral science, and they operate in the US where the startup is likely based.

pins.name: Norwest Equity Partners
pins.compatibility_summary: Norwest has a dedicated focus on health and wellness startups with a $1.8T market opportunity, directly relevant to accountability solutions for New Year's resolutions. While they typically invest at Series A and beyond, their consumer health focus and significant check sizes make them suitable for growth-stage funding needs.

pins.name: SOSV
pins.compatibility_summary: SOSV invests across healthcare and deep tech with a strong track record in digital therapeutics. Their HAX accelerator program has supported health tech startups, making them well-suited for a tech-driven accountability solution. Their check size range aligns well with typical seed-stage funding requirements of $1-3M.

pins.name: General Catalyst
pins.stage_focus: Seed, Series A, Series B, Series C
pins.compatibility_summary: General Catalyst has invested in mental health startups like Modern Health and has a strong healthcare practice. Managing director Holly Maloney specifically focuses on health and wellness startups, making them highly relevant for a behavioral accountability solution targeting the 92% resolution failure problem.

pins.name: Anthem Venture Partners
pins.compatibility_summary: Anthem Venture Partners focuses exclusively on healthcare investments with a consumer health emphasis. Their portfolio includes companies like Noom (behavioral psychology-based weight loss), making them an ideal match for accountability tech leveraging behavioral science to solve resolution failures.
